LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ' MOTION FOR TERMINATING DISCOVERY SANCTIONS AGAINST EDWARD L. NOVAK - PLAINTIFF HAS DEMONSTRATED THAT DEFENDANT EDWARD NOVAK HAS WILLFULLY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH FOUR PRIOR COURT ORDERS AGAINST HIM. ON JUNE 9, 2014, THE COURT ORDERED DEFENDANT TO ANSWER DEPOSITION QUESTIONS AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER. ON APRIL 7, 2014, THE COURT ORDERED DEFENDANT TO ANSWER INTERROGATORIES AND PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER. ON APRIL 7, 2014, THE COURT ORDERED DEFENDANT TO RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION AND PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER. ON APRIL 7, 2014, PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS WERE DEEMED ADMITTED AND DEFENDANT WAS ORDERED TO PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER. PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 2023.030, THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT'S ANSWER IS HEREBY STRICKEN AND DEFAULT IS ENTERED AGAINST DEFENDANT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/EG

PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ' MOTION FOR RENEWAL OF JUDGMENT AND DETERMINATION OF AMOUNTS OWED BY DEFENDANTS IN RENEWAL OF JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff ELVIA JUAREZ' Motion For Renewal Of Judgment And Determination Of Amounts Owed By Defendants In Renewal Of Judgment.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Elvia Juarez's Motion to Renew Judgment is ordered OFF CALENDAR. Involvement of a bench officer is unwarranted at this time. A judgment creditor seeking to renew a judgment must file a timely and complete application to renew. (See Code Civ. Proc., 683.130 [timing requirements] & 683.140 [content requirements].) In the case of a money judgment, a complete application includes "the information necessary to compute the amount of the judgment as renewed." (Code Civ. Proc., 683.140, subd. (d).) Upon the filing of such an application, "the court clerk shall enter the renewal of the judgment in the court records." (Code Civ. Proc., 683.150.) The judgment creditor must give notice of renewal. (See Code Civ. Proc., 683.160.) A judgment debtor may object by filing a timely noticed motion to vacate. (See Code Civ. Proc., 683.170, subd. (a).) "Upon the hearing of [debtor's] motion, the renewal may be ordered vacated . . ., and another and different renewal may be entered, including, but not limited to, the renewal of the judgment in a different amount if the decision of the court is that the judgment creditor is entitled to renewal in a different amount." (Code Civ. Proc., 683.170, subd. (c).)

Defendant Edward Novak'S Motion Of "Demand Motion" And "Demand Motion" For Protective Order And For Sanctions At Misused And Abusive Discovery Devices
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Edward Novak'S Motion Of "Demand Motion" And "Demand Motion" For Protective Order And For Sanctions At Misused And Abusive Discovery Devices is Denied. Defendant moves for a prospective protective order pertaining that certain matters not be inquired into. Prospective protective orders are rarely granted. Attorney-client communications or attorney work-product are in general not discoverable. However, there are exceptions. Until such time as potentially privileged communications or privileged work product are sought by Plaintiff through a formal discovery, there is no basis to rule on whether any exception applies. The denial is without prejudice to Defendant objecting or filing a subsequent motion for protective order if and when a discovery is served seeking the same. John E. Stringer, Esq. of the Law Office of John E. Stringer, Tel. 415.934.1827, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: Pursuant to Cannon 6D(5) of the California Code of Judicial Conduct, Pro Tem Judge Halperin discloses that he was a law partner in a firm in private practice with a cousin of Plaintiff's counsel Lawrence Scancarelli until December of 2007. He does not believe this fact requires recusal under California Code of Judicial Ethics Cannon 6(D)(3), but encourages the parties to raise any concerns they may have regarding this disclosure prior to the hearing. No questions were asked nor objections raised.

The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ' Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions is GRANTED. In response to a motion brought under C.C.P. Section 2033-280(b) to deem admitted the truth of matters specified in requests for admissions, a court shall make such an order unless it finds that the party to whom the requests for admission have been directed has served, before the hearing on the motion, a proposed response to the requests for admissions that is in substantial compliance with CCP Section 2033.220. Defendant Anthony Novak has provided no evidence of any such response to Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions - Set One. The truth of the matters specified in Plaintiff's requests for admissions numbers 1 through 8 are DEEMED ADMITTED. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $1,075.00. Defendant Anthony Novak shall pay $1,075.00 to Plaintiff Elvia Juarez within 30 days of notice of entry of order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
